nina.name: Nina Hu
nina.qualifications: Nina has a Bachelor of Musculoskeletal Health and a Postgraduate Diploma in Osteopathy. She has also completed training in Postgraduate Certificate in Health reputation for successful treatment outcomes in clinic.
nina.approach: During Nina's childhood, she experienced delayed speech development, a challenge including energy healing, Naturopathy, and Acupuncture to address this issue. Despite not being excel in verbal, intuitive feeling and palpation.
nina.specialInterests: When Nina feels stressed or low energy levels, she does Meditation and Qigong to restore her energy and calm her central nervous system. Serinces with her clients.
nina.aboutMe: Nina loves sports and often be found engaging in activities such Badminton, Skiing in the mountains, and Kayaking in the Bay. With her first-hand experience of sports-related injuries, she empathizes with the frustrations they bring and offers custom address them effectively.

jonathan.name: Jonathan Grice
jonathan.qualifications: Jonathan trained at the British College of Osteopathic Medicine in London and is a NZ Registered Osteopath. After graduating, he built extensive clinical experience in private practice from 2010, before holding an NHS contract in Twickenham where he received GP referrals. From 2019 to 2022 he worked in advanced clinical practice at an NHS GP surgery, focusing on musculoskeletal diagnosis and onward referral. Born and raised in Christchurch, Jonathan returned home in 2022 — bringing with him over a decade of international experience, including a period as a vaccinator during the COVID-19 pandemic.
jonathan.approach: Jonathan's treatments draw on structural, classical, and cranial osteopathic techniques, tailored to each patient's presentation and goals. Alongside hands-on osteopathy, he incorporates bio-photomodulation (low-level laser therapy) for pain management and tissue healing — an area in which he has developed particular expertise through his work with THOR Photomedicine, where he authored over 200 treatment protocols. He also uses kinesiology taping and exercise rehabilitation where clinically appropriate.
jonathan.specialInterests: Jonathan has a deep specialist interest in bio-photomodulation and its clinical applications in musculoskeletal and chronic pain conditions. His work with THOR Photomedicine has seen him develop protocols for a wide range of presentations — from aphthous ulcers to post-herpetic neuralgia. He also brings extensive experience managing complex musculoskeletal cases from his years working within NHS general practice.
jonathan.aboutMe: Born and raised in Christchurch, Jonathan spent many years working in the UK before returning home in 2022. He is glad to be back in the city he grew up in, and brings a broad clinical perspective shaped by years of NHS practice and international collaboration. Outside the clinic, Jonathan remains connected to his community and enjoys the outdoor lifestyle that Christchurch and the surrounding region have to offer.

maddison.name: Maddison Moffat
maddison.qualifications: Maddison completed a Bachelor of Musculoskeletal Health and a Postgraduate Diploma in Osteopathy at Ara Institute of Canterbury. She is registered with the Osteopathic Council of New Zealand and Osteopaths New Zealand, and is an ACC-registered provider. Born and raised in Christchurch, Maddison developed a passion for understanding the human body and movement from an early age through her lifelong involvement in sport.
maddison.approach: Maddison embraces osteopathy's holistic philosophy, providing individualised, patient-centred care that considers the whole body rather than just the site of pain or injury. She works with patients across a broad spectrum — from acute injuries to long-standing chronic conditions — adapting her approach to suit each person's needs, history, and goals. Her aim is always to help people return to the activities they love.
maddison.specialInterests: Maddison has a particular interest in sports-related injuries and movement-based complaints, informed by her own active lifestyle and lifelong connection to sport. She also has a genuine enthusiasm for working with children, bringing both care and patience to paediatric appointments. Whether supporting an athlete through recovery or helping a patient manage ongoing pain, Maddison brings the same commitment and warmth to every consultation.
maddison.aboutMe: Outside the clinic, Maddison lives an active life — you'll find her at the gym, out walking, or cheering on her favourite sports teams. A proud Christchurch local, she loves being part of a community-focused practice where she can make a real difference to people's everyday lives. She believes that a strong therapeutic relationship is the foundation of good care, and works hard to make every patient feel genuinely supported.

min.name: Min Jin
min.qualifications: Min holds a Bachelor of Clinical Medicine from Gansu University of Chinese Medicine (2002) and a Master of Biomedical Project Management from the University of Science and Technology of China (2013). In 2025, she completed a Bachelor's degree at the New Zealand School of Acupuncture and Traditional Chinese Medicine, which included over 320 hours of supervised clinical practice. Min is a registered member of the Chinese Medicine Council of New Zealand (CMCNZ). Before establishing her acupuncture practice in New Zealand, Min spent many years in clinical research — including four years at Peking University Stem Cell Research Centre and over a decade managing nationally approved clinical projects in areas such as Parkinson's disease, spinal cord injury, and oncology.
min.approach: Min provides safe, evidence-informed, and patient-centred care, weaving together classical Chinese medicine theory with Tung-style acupuncture techniques. She takes time to understand each patient's full picture — physical, emotional, and lifestyle — before forming a treatment plan. Alongside acupuncture, she draws on moxibustion, cupping, and therapeutic massage where clinically appropriate, tailoring every session to the individual.
min.specialInterests: Min's clinical focus includes musculoskeletal pain (neck, shoulder, and back), headaches and migraines, digestive complaints, sleep difficulties, stress-related symptoms, and women's health — including menstrual irregularities and menopausal symptoms. Her background in clinical research gives her a deep appreciation for evidence-based practice, which she brings together with the wisdom of traditional Chinese medicine to offer a uniquely informed approach to care.
min.aboutMe: Min's path to acupuncture is shaped by a rich background in both science and medicine. Her years in clinical research gave her a profound respect for evidence-based thinking, which she combines with a genuine belief in the healing potential of traditional Chinese medicine. Outside of work, Min values balance — she enjoys time in nature, nourishing food, and the quieter rhythms of everyday life. She brings patience, warmth, and genuine curiosity to every consultation.

consultationSteps.title: Initial Consultation
consultationSteps.description: Consent form, case history, physical examination, treatment plan discussion. 45–60 minutes. Gentle hands-on treatment including balanced ligamentous tension, soft tissue techniques, and myofascial unwinding.

consultationSteps.title: Follow-up Treatments
consultationSteps.description: Tailored to your response. May include different osteopathic techniques, dry needling, auricular/electrical acupuncture, and cupping based on progress.

consultationSteps.title: Rehabilitation
consultationSteps.description: Personalised rehab via Physitrack app — exercise videos sent to your phone. Designed to prevent future injuries and support long-term recovery.
